idea-plugin.name: Method Timer
idea-plugin.description:
Method Timer — Automatic Method Execution Time Profiling

Automatically measures Java method execution time and displays results as Code Vision lenses above method declarations in the editor.

Features

  • Zero configuration — no annotations or code changes required
  • User code only — libraries and frameworks are never instrumented
  • Real-time updates — timings refresh every 3 seconds while the app is running
  • Console and server apps — works with short scripts and Spring Boot alike
  • Persistent data — results are saved between IDE restarts
  • Clickable lenses — click on timing to see method details

How it works

When you run a Java application from the IDE, the plugin automatically attaches a Java Agent that instruments your methods with timing hooks. Results are displayed as Code Vision lenses (like "N usages") above each method.

Limitations

  • Works with direct Java/JUnit runs only (not Gradle-delegated)
  • Java methods only (not Kotlin)
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.1.0

  • Fixed generic method signature matching (type erasure)
  • Fixed thread safety of timing storage after IDE restart
  • Fixed scheduler thread leak on plugin unload
  • Fixed potential PSI access without ReadAction
  • Removed duplicate Code Vision provider registration

1.0.0

  • Initial release
  • Java Agent with ByteBuddy instrumentation
  • Code Vision integration for timing display
  • Real-time polling for long-running processes
  • Persistent timing storage across IDE sessions
